summary: Matched and enabled linking for `src/melee/it/items/itsamusbomb.c`. The PR marked the file as `Object(Matching, ...)`, emitted the Samus bomb item state table in C, adjusted local variable/layout details for exact codegen, changed one Samus bomb owner callsite, and converted several item constants/strings in `symbols.txt` from global address symbols to local `@NNN` labels. Evidence for intent is mostly the title, diff, and decomp-dev bot report; there was no PR body or human review discussion.
what_changed: `itsamusbomb.c` gained `ItemStateTable it_803F7220[]` with four motion entries using `itSamusbomb_UnkMotion0/1/2/3` callbacks and `NULL` phys/coll entries for motion 3.
`itsamusbomb.c` moved `baselib/mtx.h` from a quoted include to `<baselib/mtx.h>`.
`my_sqrtf` in `itsamusbomb.c` gained an explicit `u8 _[4] = { 0 };` local before `volatile float y`, consistent with stack-layout/codegen matching work.
`itSamusbomb_UnkMotion2_Phys` hoisted `f32 mult` out of the `if` block before the velocity clamp logic.
`it_802B5478` now passes `ip->xDD4_itemVar.samusbomb.owner` to `ftSs_Init_80128A1C` instead of passing the current item `gobj`.
`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` converted several item data/sdata/sdata2 entries such as `it_803F7260`, `it_804D54C8`, and `it_804DD018` into local `@NNN` labels with `scope:local`.
`itlikelike.c` normalized `baselib/gobj.h` to an angle-bracket include placed with other baselib includes.
smart_moves: The PR handled both code and data matching: the bot reported new 100% matches for `itsamusbomb` `.data`, `.sdata2`, `itSamusbomb_UnkMotion2_Phys`, and `it_802B5478`.
Adding the real `ItemStateTable it_803F7220[]` in C replaced symbol-only data with a typed initializer while preserving callback order, flags, and `NULL` entries.
The symbol-map edits avoided fake global names for compiler-local constants/strings by using local `@NNN` labels, which is a reusable tactic for matched data sections.
Small source-shape changes were kept targeted: a 4-byte dummy local in `my_sqrtf` and a `f32 mult` declaration-scope change rather than broad rewrites.
The `ftSs_Init_80128A1C` call was made consistent with the surrounding owner-based Samus bomb logic; the next call in the same block already used `ip->xDD4_itemVar.samusbomb.owner`.
decomp_lessons: When an item object becomes matching, remember to flip its `configure.py` entry from `NonMatching` to `Matching`; otherwise the matched source will not be treated as part of the matching build.
For item state tables, preserve exact row order, callback names, state flags, and `NULL` function pointers; these details can be enough to recover `.data` matching.
Compiler-emitted local strings/floats/doubles should often be represented as `@NNN` local labels in `symbols.txt` instead of promoted `it_...` globals.
Declaration placement can matter for matching even when C semantics are unchanged; hoisting a local variable can alter stack/register allocation.
Dummy locals may be necessary for stack layout, but should be minimal and localized, as shown by `u8 _[4] = { 0 };` in `my_sqrtf`.
Object identity matters in function arguments: passing an owner fighter GObj instead of the item GObj can be both codegen-significant and consistent with article ownership logic.
naming_conventions: Samus bomb callbacks use the `itSamusbomb_` prefix with `UnkMotionN` motion numbering and `_Anim`, `_Phys`, `_Coll` suffixes.
Unrenamed functions remained address-style, including `it_802B4AC8` and `it_802B5478`.
The known state table kept the address-style data name `it_803F7220`.
Anonymous local constants/strings in `symbols.txt` use `@NNN` labels such as `@244`, `@245`, `@246`, `@247`, `@324`, and `@325` with `scope:local`.
Baselib headers in these edits were expressed with angle brackets, e.g. `<baselib/mtx.h>` and `<baselib/gobj.h>`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Emit data tables directly in C once their structure and callback order are known; this produced a full `.data` match for `itsamusbomb` according to the bot report.
Use local `@NNN` symbol labels for compiler-local constants to improve data/link matching without inventing global names.
Use narrowly scoped stack-layout nudges, such as a 4-byte dummy local before a volatile float, when needed for exact function matching.
Try declaration-lifetime changes before semantic rewrites; moving `f32 mult` outside the branch helped the `itSamusbomb_UnkMotion2_Phys` match.
Check whether callee arguments should be parent/owner GObjs rather than the current item GObj; the `ftSs_Init_80128A1C` change contributed to matching `it_802B5478`.
review_feedback: No human reviews or review comments were present in the PR slice, and the PR body was null.
decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 54.50% (+336 bytes), linked code 31.10% (+2640 bytes), matched data 34.52% (+136 bytes), and linked data 28.89% (+152 bytes).
decomp-dev bot listed 4 new matches: `main/melee/it/items/itsamusbomb` `.data`, `main/melee/it/items/itsamusbomb` `.sdata2`, `itSamusbomb_UnkMotion2_Phys`, and `it_802B5478`.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining `melee/it/items/*.c` entries in `configure.py` that are still `Object(NonMatching, ...)` and may only need data-table or small codegen fixes.
Search `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` for item-module `scope:global` strings/floats/doubles that should become local `@NNN` labels after matching.
Audit Samus article callsites for `ftSs_Init_80128A1C` and `ftSs_Init_80128944` to confirm owner GObj versus item GObj usage.
Look for other item files with missing `ItemStateTable` definitions where preserving callback order could recover `.data` matches.
Compare other near-matching functions that may need declaration-scope or dummy-local stack-layout adjustments similar to `my_sqrtf` and `itSamusbomb_UnkMotion2_Phys`.
